summary: Regression test for Code Quality Round 2 fixes. All 8 backend endpoints pass. Fixed a critical frontend compile error caused by em-dash (—) in eslint-disable comments in DetailPanel.jsx and ContactDetailPanel.jsx — ESLint was treating text after the em-dash as invalid rule names, causing a 'Compiled with problems' overlay on every page load. Fixed to use double-dash (--) syntax. After fix, ConnectPage loads cleanly with no compile errors.

ui_bugs.issue: eslint-disable-line comments used em-dash (—) instead of double-dash (--) for inline description. ESLint treated words after em-dash as rule names, causing 'Compiled with problems' overlay blocking the entire app.

critical_code_review_comments: ESLint inline disable comment format: always use double-dash (--) not em-dash (—) when adding explanations: // eslint-disable-line rule-name -- explanation
context_for_next_testing_agent: All backend APIs working. ConnectPage renders for unauthenticated users. Settings Promise.all confirmed present at line 54. Dashboard polling catch block verified at line 218-225. DetailPanel has no console.log. Critical fix applied: eslint-disable em-dash → double-dash in DetailPanel.jsx (line 103) and ContactDetailPanel.jsx (line 48).
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause: When adding human-readable explanations to ESLint disable comments, code used Unicode em-dash (—) as separator. ESLint's comment parser only recognizes '--' (double hyphen) as a separator between rule names and description. Everything after '—' was parsed as additional rule names (e.g. 'intentional:', 'reset', 'local', etc.) which don't exist, causing compilation errors. Fix: replace '—' with '--' in all eslint-disable comments.
